basic_details.job_title: GSRTC Conductor Bharti 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 4318 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Gujarat State Road Transport Corporation (GSRTC), Central Office, Ranip, Ahmedabad
basic_details.short_information: GSRTC is recruiting 4318 Conductor posts on a fixed pay contract basis with Class 12 pass qualification and valid Conductor Licence, last date 05 July 2026. Daily Current Affairs 24 June 2026: Download Free PDF Now

application_fee.details: Fee must be paid online via https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in. Fee once paid is non-refundable under any circumstances, including if the recruitment is cancelled or modified.

age_limit.details: Minimum age: 18 years. Age is calculated as on the last date of application (05-07-2026). Under no circumstances shall the maximum age (with all relaxations) exceed 45 years for any category. | Age Relaxation Details: General Female candidates get 5 years additional relaxation. All reserved category Male candidates get 5 years relaxation. All reserved category Female candidates get 10 years relaxation. General Divyang Male candidates get 10 years relaxation. General Divyang Female candidates get 15 years relaxation. Reserved Divyang Male candidates get 15 years relaxation. Reserved Divyang Female candidates get 20 years relaxation. Ex-Servicemen are entitled to relaxation equal to the period of military service rendered plus 3 additional years (maximum up to 45 years). Maximum age with all relaxations must not exceed 45 years under any circumstances.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of application i.e., 05-07-2026. All original certificates of educational qualification, age, caste, experience, and other qualifications must be in possession at the time of application.
qualification.education_qualification: Class 12 Pass (10+2) from Gujarat Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Board or any equivalent recognized board. Diploma (10+3 or more years after Class 10) is also accepted. ITI (2-year or more NCVT-passed course after Class 10 + English subject passed from GSEB or Gujarat Open School Examination) and 3-year Polytechnic Diploma after Class 10 are also treated as equivalent to Class 12 for government service purposes (with equivalence certificate from Education Board).
qualification.nationality: Indian citizen; OR subject of Nepal/Bhutan; OR Tibetan refugee who came to India before 01/01/1962 with intention of permanent settlement; OR person of Indian origin who migrated from Pakistan, Bangladesh, Burma, Sri Lanka, East African countries etc. for permanent settlement in India (eligibility certificate from Government required for the latter categories)

vacancy_details.Disability Type: Leprosy Cured (LC), Acid Attack Victim (AAV); OA, OL, BA, BL, OAL, BLOA, BLA (subject to Medical Board opinion)

pwd_wise_breakdown.disability_type: Leprosy Cured (LC), Acid Attack Victim (AAV); OA, OL, BA, BL, OAL, BLOA, BLA (subject to Medical Board opinion)

selection_process: Selection will be based on merit in the OMR-based Competitive Written Examination. All applicants will be called for the 100-mark OMR Written Examination. Based on merit

candidates will be shortlisted at a ratio of 1.5 candidates per vacancy for Document Verification. A Provisional Selection List and Waiting List will be prepared after document verification. The Waiting List will remain valid for 2 years from date of publication or until the result of the next recruitment examination for this post - whichever is earlier.
Stage 1 – OMR Written Examination: 100 marks / 100 questions / 1 hour. All registered candidates will be called. No minimum qualifying marks; however

candidates scoring 0 (zero) or negative marks will not be eligible for document verification. Stage 2 – Document Verification: Candidates shortlisted at a 1:1.5 ratio per vacancy based on written exam merit will be called. Provisional Selection List prepared after document verification. Special Weightage: Widow candidates get 5% additional marks added to their OMR score at all stages for priority consideration (subject to not having remarried). Sports persons (who have represented at National/International

or AISS level in specified sports) also get 5% additional marks on OMR score. Performance Review: Appointed candidates on contract will have their performance reviewed after 2 years; continuation for remaining period is subject to satisfactory performance. After successful completion of 5 years
candidates become eligible for regular appointment as per the Corporation's prevailing pay scale.

pattern_table.Subject: General Knowledge / History of Gujarat / Geography / Current Affairs of Gujarat (Class 12 level)
Quantitative Aptitude and Test of Reasoning (Class 12 level)
Information related to GSRTC / Mathematical questions on Ticket and Luggage Fare calculation
Basic knowledge of Motor Vehicle Act / First Aid / Duties of Conductors

exam_pattern.details: OMR-based Competitive Written Examination Pattern: | The question paper will be based on Class 12 standard (Gujarat Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Board). Medium of examination: Gujarati for all subjects except English Grammar (which will be in English). 1 mark per question; 100 questions in total; duration 1 hour. Negative marking: 0.25 marks deducted for each wrong answer, blank/unattempted, overwritten, or multiple-option responses. If the candidate marks option (E) on the OMR sheet to indicate they do not wish to answer, no negative marking will be applied for that question. Candidates must use Blue or Black ballpoint pen only.

syllabus.details: OMR-based Competitive Written Examination Pattern: | Subject Marks General Knowledge / History of Gujarat / Geography / Current Affairs of Gujarat (Class 12 level) 20 Road Safety 10 Gujarati Grammar (Class 12 level) 10 English Grammar (Class 12 level) 10 Quantitative Aptitude and Test of Reasoning (Class 12 level) 10 Information related to GSRTC / Mathematical questions on Ticket and Luggage Fare calculation 10 Basic knowledge of Motor Vehicle Act / First Aid / Duties of Conductors 10 Basic Computer Usage knowledge 20 Total 100 | The question paper will be based on Class 12 standard (Gujarat Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Board). Medium of examination: Gujarati for all subjects except English Grammar (which will be in English). 1 mark per question; 100 questions in total; duration 1 hour. Negative marking: 0.25 marks deducted for each wrong answer, blank/unattempted, overwritten, or multiple-option responses. If the candidate marks option (E) on the OMR sheet to indicate they do not wish to answer, no negative marking will be applied for that question. Candidates must use Blue or Black ballpoint pen only.

how_to_apply: Candidates must apply online only through https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must upload their own photograph and signature; applications with mismatched/invalid photo or signature will be rejected.
Step 1: Visit https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in → Click "Apply Online" → Click on "Conductor" post to view vacancy details.
Step 2: Click "Apply Now" → Fill Personal Details (fields with * are mandatory).
Step 3: Click "Educational Qualification" → Fill minimum educational details. Click "Computer Knowledge" → Fill computer qualification details.
Step 4: Click "Self Declaration" → Accept terms by clicking "Yes" → Click "Save" to complete online application submission.
Step 5: Upload Photo (JPG, max 10 KB; size 5 cm height x 3.6 cm width) and Signature (JPG, max 10 KB; size 2.5 cm height x 7.5 cm width).

Step 6: Click "Confirm Application" → Review application via "Show Application Preview" → Edit if required → Click "Confirm Application" to finalize. Note the Confirmation Number generated for all future reference.
Step 7: Click "Online Payment of Fees" → Enter Confirmation Number and Date of Birth → Pay fee through Online Payment Gateway.
Step 8: Click "Print Application" → Enter Confirmation Number and Date of Birth → Print and save the application for your records.
important_instructions: Only online applications via https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in will be accepted; no other mode will be entertained. If multiple applications are submitted, only the last submitted (final) application will be considered; all previous applications will be automatically cancelled. Fee paid for previous applications will not be refunded. If the last submitted application has no fee payment, that application along with all previous ones will be rejected. Candidates in GSRTC / Government / Semi-Government service must inform their department/office within 7 days of applying and must obtain and submit a NOC at the time of document verification. The date of birth as per SSC Board Certificate or School Leaving Certificate will be the final accepted date of birth; any subsequent change will not be permitted. Birth extract/certificate is NOT accepted. Class 12 qualifications, Conductor Licence, Badge, Computer certificate, caste certificates, and all other documents must have been obtained before the last date of application; documents dated after the last date will not be accepted. Only original Gujarat domicile residents of SC/ST/SEBC/EWS categories will be entitled to reservation benefits. Candidates from other states are not entitled to reservation. Reservation category once mentioned in the application form cannot be changed later. If the caste stated in the application does not match the certificate, the application will be rejected - even if the candidate is eligible under the General category. Candidates must not bring any handwritten/printed material, calculator, mobile phone, smart watch, earphone, earbuds, or any electronic device to the examination hall; violation will result in disqualification. Any identification mark, writing, or alphabet in the OMR answer sheet will result in disqualification from the examination. Inclusion in the final Selection List does not automatically entitle a candidate to appointment; the appointing authority's decision regarding suitability is final. If any information provided in the application is found to be false, the application will be cancelled at any stage, and the Corporation reserves the right to take legal action. Candidates with pending criminal cases at any court of law at the time of appointment will have their applications rejected. RTI applications for recruitment-related information will not be entertained until the entire recruitment process is completed. Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Ahmedabad only. GSRTC reserves the right to cancel or modify the recruitment process at any stage without assigning reasons; in such cases, the application fee will not be refunded.

_labels.notification_pdf: Download GSRTC Conductor Bharti 2026 Official Notification PDF

all_official_links.label: Download GSRTC Conductor Bharti 2026 Official Notification PDF

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies for GSRTC Conductor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 4318 Conductor posts have been announced by Gujarat State Road Transport Corporation (GSRTC) for direct recruitment on a fixed pay contract basis. GSRTC reserves the right to increase or decrease the number of vacancies.

faq.question: Q2. What is the fixed pay for the GSRTC Conductor post?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be appointed on a contract basis with a fixed pay of Rs. 26,000/- per month for a period of 5 years. After satisfactory completion of 5 years of service, candidates become eligible for regular appointment as per GSRTC's prevailing pay scale for the Conductor post. No other allowances or benefits will be payable during the contract period except as per rules.

faq.question: Q3. What are the essential qualifications for GSRTC Conductor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Class 12 (10+2) from GSEB or an equivalent recognized board, must hold a valid Conductor Licence issued by the Regional Transport Authority (RTA) along with a Badge, and must possess a Basic Computer Knowledge certificate as per the prescribed Gujarat Government resolution. All these must be valid as on the last date of application (05-07-2026).

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for GSRTC Conductor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age for General (Male) candidates is 35 years as on the last date of application (05-07-2026). General Female candidates can apply up to 40 years; Reserved category Male candidates up to 40 years; Reserved category Female candidates up to 45 years; Ex-Servicemen and Divyang (PwD) candidates up to 45 years. Under no circumstances shall the age with all relaxations exceed 45 years.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for GSRTC Conductor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: All registered candidates will be called for a 100-mark OMR-based Competitive Written Examination of 1-hour duration. Based on merit in the written exam, candidates will be shortlisted at a ratio of 1.5 per vacancy for document verification. A Provisional Selection List and Waiting List (at 20% of vacancies, category-wise) will be prepared after document verification. There are no minimum qualifying marks, but candidates scoring zero or negative will not be eligible for document verification.

faq.question: Q6. Is there negative marking in the GSRTC Conductor written examination?
faq.answer: Yes. For every wrong answer, blank/skipped response, overwritten answer, or multiple options marked, 0.25 marks will be deducted. However, if a candidate marks option (E) on the OMR sheet indicating they do not wish to answer that question, no negative marking will be applied for that particular question.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for GSRTC Conductor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: General (Unreserved) candidates must pay Rs. 300/- + GST. All reserved category candidates (SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, Divyang, Ex-Servicemen — both Male and Female) must pay Rs. 200/- + GST. Fee must be paid online via https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in and is non-refundable under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q8. Can widow and sports persons get additional marks in GSRTC Conductor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes. Widow candidates who have not remarried are entitled to 5% additional marks on their OMR written examination score at all stages of selection for priority consideration. Sports persons who have represented at National/International level, Inter-University level, or All India School Sports Federation level in the specified 25 sports disciplines are also entitled to 5% additional marks on their OMR score. Relevant certificates from competent authorities must be produced at document verification, failing which the additional marks will not be awarded.

faq.question: Q9. What types of PwD (Divyang) disabilities are eligible for GSRTC Conductor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The following disability categories are eligible: Low Vision (LV) 40–70% (Category A), Hard of Hearing (HH) 40–70% (Category B), Leprosy Cured (LC), Acid Attack Victim (AAV), OA/OL/BA/BL/OAL/BLOA/BLA type locomotor disabilities subject to Medical Board opinion (Category C), and Mental Illness (MI) 40–70% (Category D & E). Disability certificate must be from a Government Hospital Superintendent / Civil Surgeon / Medical Board and must be held as on the date of online application. Divyang candidates selected through the Divyang quota will not be entitled to future inter-class transfer policy benefits in GSRTC.

faq.question: Q10. How long will the GSRTC Conductor 2026 Waiting List remain valid?
faq.answer: The Waiting List (Pratiksha Yadi) will remain valid for 2 years from the date of its publication OR until the result of the next recruitment examination for the Conductor post is declared — whichever is earlier. A category-wise waiting list will be prepared at 20% of the total vacancies. After this period, the waiting list will automatically stand cancelled. GSRTC reserves the right to modify the percentage of waiting list if required.

title: GSRTC Conductor Bharti 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 4318 Posts
